generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance InputCurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Physical sensor measuring input current of the robot drive, providing real‑time current feedback."
    DTDFVocab:producedData InputCurrentDataTransmitted
]

instance MotorAngularVelocitySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor measuring motor angular velocity of each joint, used for friction estimation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData AngularVelocityDataTransmitted
]

instance FirstAxisPositionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor measuring the position of the robot's first axis, required for kinematic context."
    DTDFVocab:producedData AxisPositionDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction (DataTransmitted)
instance InputCurrentDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InputCurrentSensor
]

instance AngularVelocityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorAngularVelocitySensor
]

instance AxisPositionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom FirstAxisPositionSensor
]

// C10: Data generated from transmitted sensor streams
instance InputCurrentData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Low‑pass filtered current value, formatted according to the AAS data model."
    DTDFVocab:fromData InputCurrentDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelExecutionEngine
]

instance AngularVelocityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Filtered motor angular velocity, enriched with timestamp and joint identifier."
    DTDFVocab:fromData AngularVelocityDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelExecutionEngine
]

instance AxisPositionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Filtered position of the first axis, ready for kinematic calculations."
    DTDFVocab:fromData AxisPositionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelExecutionEngine
]

// C11: Enablers (processing components)
instance ModelExecutionEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Execution engine that consumes pre‑processed sensor data, runs physics‑based (white‑box) and neural‑network (black‑box) friction models, and emits derived data traces."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionParameterIdentificationService,
                     ModelRetrainingService,
                     FrictionCompensationService,
                     AnomalyDetectionService,
                     SimulationOptimizationService,
                     CentralDataMemoryService
]

// C10: Models/Data (computational models)
instance PhysicsBasedFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Physics‑based (white‑box) friction model implemented in Simulink, representing joint dynamics."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelExecutionEngine
]

instance DataDrivenFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Neural‑network (black‑box) friction model trained on historical sensor traces."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelExecutionEngine
]

// C6: Services provided by the Digital Twin
instance FrictionParameterIdentificationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuously identifies friction parameters from incoming sensor data using both white‑box and black‑box models."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionParameterInsight
]

instance ModelRetrainingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automatically updates or retrains the physics‑based and data‑driven friction models when new data indicate drift."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelRetrainingAction
]

instance FrictionCompensationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Supplies on‑line friction compensation values to the robot controller in real time."
    DTDFVocab:provides ApplyFrictionCompensationAction
]

instance AnomalyDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Detects anomalies in friction behavior for early fault detection and predictive maintenance."
    DTDFVocab:provides AnomalyInsight,
                     AnomalyAlertAction
]

instance SimulationOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Enables simulation‑based optimisation of machining parameters before production."
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimizationRecommendationInsight,
                     UpdateProcessParametersAction
]

instance CentralDataMemoryService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides a central data memory that can be reused by downstream applications such as CO₂‑reduction optimisation or product‑quality prediction."
    DTDFVocab:provides CentralDataRepositoryInsight,
                     DataReuseAction
]

// C17: Insights
instance FrictionParameterInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Derived friction parameter values together with provenance for use in compensation and monitoring."
]

instance AnomalyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight indicating detected friction anomalies with traceability to the originating sensor traces."
]

instance OptimizationRecommendationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Performance indicator suggesting optimal machining parameters based on simulation results."
]

instance CentralDataRepositoryInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Overview of stored data assets that can be accessed by downstream analytics (e.g., CO₂‑reduction or quality prediction)."
]

// C17: Actions
instance ModelRetrainingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic retraining of friction models using the latest sensor data."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ApplyFrictionCompensationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic injection of computed friction compensation values into the control loop."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance AnomalyAlertAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Raises an alert to the operator when a friction anomaly is detected."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance UpdateProcessParametersAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Updates machining process parameters based on optimisation recommendations."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance DataReuseAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Enables downstream applications to query and reuse stored digital‑shadow data."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The digital shadow ingests a continuous sensor data stream from the robot; processing must satisfy the real‑time requirements of the robot controller, i.e., operate within the controller’s dead‑time so that joint‑friction identification can be performed during the same control cycle. The temporal granularity corresponds to the robot’s control‑cycle sampling (per‑axis measurement) and updates are performed at runtime."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The system is orchestrated as a pipeline: live robot data are collected via an Asset Administration Shell (AAS) and transported with MQTT to a ThingsBoard data store; an AAS Executor triggers executable simulation models (SysML structural model and Simulink friction models, integrated through the Functional Mock‑up Interface) that compute friction estimates; the results are written back to the digital shadow in ThingsBoard and pushed to a visualization dashboard. Coordination is achieved through the AAS‑based configuration and MQTT messaging, while the processing component consumes input traces and produces output traces as defined in the reference model."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The development of a Digital Twin follows the Model‑Based Systems‑Engineering (MBSE) lifecycle that is split into four abstraction levels: (1) requirements, (2) functional architecture, (3) logical architecture, and (4) product/technical layer. In the early stages a purpose‑driven digital‑shadow design starts from the intended use‑case and derives the required data traces; alternatively a data‑driven approach starts from the available sensor data and builds the shadow upwards. The Digital Shadow Reference Model provides a reference CD that is concretized through a conformance relation, allowing traceability of each concrete element back to its abstract concept. Transition between lifecycle phases is managed by the “originates‑from” association, which records the processing component, input traces and models that generated each data trace, thereby preserving transitive traceability and supporting governance of model updates and model‑driven evolution."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Model fidelity is maintained by traceability links that record the origin of each data trace and the transformations applied (e.g., SysML → simulation models). The approach stresses verification of model transformations to ensure correctness, as reliability of the digital shadow depends on the underlying models and data pipelines. Continuous data streams enable dynamic adaptation of model parameters, and the traceability infrastructure supports detection of inconsistencies and quality assurance of both white‑box and black‑box models."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "Communication between components uses the MQTT protocol in a publish‑subscribe pattern. The MQTT broker acts as the central hub, receiving preprocessed sensor data, converting them to JSON payloads and distributing them over hierarchically organized topics. No specific network topology, bandwidth, latency, reliability, redundancy, or security mechanisms are described in the documents."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The digital‑shadow implementation consists of an Asset Administration Shell (AAS) that stores the data model, an AAS Executor, an MQTT broker, and a Thingsboard instance that provides storage and a visualization dashboard. The documents do not specify whether these components are deployed on‑premise, at the edge, or in the cloud, nor do they detail computational or storage requirements, scalability approaches, or availability/reliability provisions."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Interoperability is realized using standardized protocols and platforms: MQTT provides message transport between IoT sensors, the AAS and the processing components; the Asset Administration Shell supplies a semantic wrapper for the robot’s data and models; ThingsBoard is used for IoT data storage and dashboard visualization; structural models are expressed in SysML and behavioral models in Simulink, linked via the Functional Mock‑up Interface (FMI). These standards enable integration of the digital shadow with external engineering tools and enterprise systems."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Twin utilizes standardized modelling languages for architecture description: SysML, ACME, AADL and MontiArc (Doc 1). Model conformity is enforced through a reference‑model conformance relation as defined in the Digital Shadow Reference Model (Doc 2). Communication with physical assets follows the MQTT protocol, exemplified by the Mosquitto client/server implementation (Doc 6). Interoperability at the application layer can be achieved with the open‑source ThingsBoard IoT platform (Doc 6). These standards and reference‑model mechanisms together underpin the Twin’s compliance and integration capabilities."
]
